attacking_style: Explosive attacks in final 1-5km of summit finishes on steep gradients (14-20%), often on road bike setup. Prefers very late accelerations that leverage superior power sustainability rather than long-range moves.
team_tactics: Jumbo-Visma sets prolonged tempo with domestiques (especially Van Aert pre-mountains, McNulty/Kuss on climbs) to isolate rivals before Vingegaard's final acceleration. Team willing to sacrifice positioning for stage wins, showing unusual tactical flexibility.
terrain_preference: Long sustained climbs at high altitude with steep finishing gradients (9-16%) where power-to-weight dominates. Vulnerable on punchy, explosive finishes under 3km and technical descents requiring aggressive risk-taking. TT strength on road bikes but not pure TT setup.
vulnerability: Early-season form significantly weaker than July peak (lost 43-44s in March Paris-Nice). Exposed when isolated without teammates mid-stage. Struggles to respond immediately to surprise attacks on opening climbs, requiring bridging efforts. Shows visible distress on extremely steep explosive gradients.
key_patterns: Attacks with 1-5km remaining on steep summit finishes, rarely goes long-range solo
Dominates July peak form but shows 40+ second deficits in March races
Matches rivals until final meters then accelerates, rarely attacks first on opening climbs
Willing to lead out sprinters and work as domestique despite GC status
Vulnerable when caught without teammates in mid-stage splits from aggressive rival teams
Superior recovery allows working for teammates day after major efforts
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Vuelta showed conflicting signals—dominant stage wins after initial fatigue concerns, but ultimately accepted domestique role. Tour dominance (7:29 margin) confirms July peak remains exceptional, but March weakness pattern persists.
